description: Transaction failures on card-processing traced to an expired client certificate used by the service account calling the HSM API. The HSM rejected all signing requests, causing payment authorisation to time out.
root_cause: Expired HSM client certificate caused all signing requests to be rejected.
resolution: Renewed the HSM client certificate via PKI portal; updated the Kubernetes secret and triggered a rolling restart.

description: Mutual TLS authentication between payments-gateway and the downstream card-processing service broke after a Kubernetes secret rotation. The new cert was issued by a different intermediate CA not present in the client trust store.
error_signature: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
root_cause: Kubernetes secret rotation introduced new intermediate CA not in client trust store.
resolution: Added the new intermediate CA to the client trust store; rolled out updated ConfigMap to all affected namespaces. Documented trust-store update as a mandatory step in the cert-rotation runbook.

description: payment-processor stopped consuming messages from the RabbitMQ queue. The broker had run out of disk space after a dead-letter queue grew unbounded following an upstream schema change that made all messages unparseable.
root_cause: Dead-letter queue grew unbounded after schema change; broker disk exhausted.
resolution: Purged dead-letter queue; added DLQ depth alert and schema-validation step.

description: Message lag on the notification-dispatcher Kafka topic reached 4 million events. A misconfigured consumer group caused two consumer pods to rebalance in an infinite loop, never committing offsets.
root_cause: Misconfigured consumer group caused infinite rebalance loop.
resolution: Fixed consumer group config; added liveness probe to kill stalled consumers.

description: Transaction failures on auth-service traced to an expired client certificate used by the service account calling the HSM API. The HSM rejected all signing requests, causing payment authorisation to time out.
root_cause: Expired HSM client certificate caused all signing requests to be rejected.
resolution: Renewed the HSM client certificate via PKI portal; updated the Kubernetes secret and triggered a rolling restart.

description: Connection pool to Kafka on payment-processor was exhausted after a connection leak introduced in version 2.4.1 of the internal MQ client library. Connections were not returned after transient errors.
error_signature: ConnectionPoolTimeoutError: no available connections in pool
root_cause: Connection leak in MQ client library v2.4.1 exhausted broker connection pool.
resolution: Upgraded MQ client library to v2.4.2 with connection-leak fix; added pool monitoring.

description: kyc-service integration tests passed in UAT but prod threw UnsatisfiedDependencyException on boot. A Spring Security version upgrade changed the bean wiring order; a conditional bean that existed in UAT (feature flag enabled) was absent in prod, breaking the dependency graph.
root_cause: Spring Security upgrade changed bean wiring; conditional bean absent in prod.
resolution: Added conditional bean to prod profile; added feature-flag parity check to CD gate.

description: Connection pool to ActiveMQ on fraud-event-stream was exhausted after a connection leak introduced in version 2.4.1 of the internal MQ client library. Connections were not returned after transient errors.
error_signature: ConnectionPoolTimeoutError: no available connections in pool
root_cause: Connection leak in MQ client library v2.4.1 exhausted broker connection pool.
resolution: Upgraded MQ client library to v2.4.2 with connection-leak fix; added pool monitoring.

description: The customer-api service started throwing SSL handshake failures shortly after midnight. Certificate for internal CA expired at 00:05 UTC and was not auto-renewed because the ACME client lost connectivity to the CA server during a routine network maintenance window.
root_cause: Expired internal CA certificate; ACME renewal failure during maintenance window.
resolution: Renewed the internal CA cert via ACME; restarted the affected service pods. Added cert-expiry Prometheus alert with 30-day warning threshold.

description: risk-scoring reported 'NoSuchMethodError' in prod. The commons-lang3 library was upgraded from 3.11 to 3.14 in the shared platform layer, but the risk-scoring JAR still referenced the old method signature because it was compiled against 3.11 and the fat-JAR was stale.
root_cause: commons-lang3 upgraded in shared layer; service JAR compiled against old version.
resolution: Rebuilt fat-JAR against commons-lang3 3.14; added shared-layer upgrade notification process.

description: mobile-banking-backend health-check endpoint returned 503 with 'peer certificate: No certificate signed by known CA'. A new firewall appliance inline inspection had broken TLS by presenting its own self-signed cert to backend services.
root_cause: Firewall inline TLS inspection presented self-signed cert to backends.
resolution: Configured the firewall appliance in transparent mode for internal east-west traffic. Whitelisted the internal CA on the appliance policy.

description: Post-deployment smoke tests for mobile-banking-backend reported HTTP 526 ('invalid SSL certificate'). Root cause: the deployment pipeline overwrote the prod cert with a staging cert during an environment-copy step.
error_signature: PKIX path building failed: unable to find valid certification path
root_cause: Deployment pipeline overwrote prod cert with staging cert.
resolution: Corrected the deployment pipeline's environment-copy step to skip secret overwrite. Promoted the correct prod cert from the secrets manager.

description: risk-scoring integration tests passed in UAT but prod threw UnsatisfiedDependencyException on boot. A Spring Security version upgrade changed the bean wiring order; a conditional bean that existed in UAT (feature flag enabled) was absent in prod, breaking the dependency graph.
root_cause: Spring Security upgrade changed bean wiring; conditional bean absent in prod.
resolution: Added conditional bean to prod profile; added feature-flag parity check to CD gate.

description: risk-scoring failed health checks after deployment. OpenAPI spec validation library springdoc-openapi was upgraded, and the new version required Spring Boot 3.x; the service still ran Spring Boot 2.7.x in prod, whereas UAT had already been migrated.
root_cause: springdoc-openapi upgrade required Spring Boot 3.x; prod still on 2.7.x.
resolution: Upgraded prod to Spring Boot 3.x; added Spring version matrix to CI.

description: Production rollout of loan-origination triggered ClassCastException deep in the HTTP client stack. The application server's provided Netty version (4.1.86) conflicted with the version bundled in the service JAR (4.1.100); class loading order differed between UAT and prod app servers.
root_cause: Netty version in app server (4.1.86) conflicted with bundled version (4.1.100).
resolution: Excluded bundled Netty from fat-JAR; aligned app server and service versions.

description: RabbitMQ cluster lost quorum after two of three nodes failed during a simultaneous patch window. audit-log-consumer could not connect to the broker and began buffering messages locally until disk was exhausted.
root_cause: RabbitMQ lost quorum after simultaneous patch of two nodes.
resolution: Restored RabbitMQ quorum by staggering node patches; implemented rolling-patch policy.

description: payment-processor experienced a complete message processing halt. A poison-pill message with a malformed timestamp caused the deserialisation layer to throw and the consumer to exit without re-queuing.
error_signature: KafkaException: Topic partition leader not available
root_cause: Poison-pill message crashed consumer without re-queue handling.
resolution: Added poison-pill handler: log and move to DLQ rather than crashing consumer.

description: High CPU on the ActiveMQ broker cluster caused throughput to drop 95 percent. Root cause: a compaction job and a schema-registry sync both ran concurrently during peak hours due to misconfigured cron.
root_cause: Compaction + schema-registry jobs ran concurrently causing CPU saturation.
resolution: Separated compaction and schema-sync cron jobs; added CPU-based backoff.

description: Batch job in batch-reconciliation aborted with 'IncompatibleClassChangeError'. A Lombok annotation processor version bump (1.18.26 → 1.18.30) generated code incompatible with the Kotlin version used in the same Maven module; UAT was on a different Kotlin version.
root_cause: Lombok and Kotlin version mismatch caused incompatible generated code.
resolution: Aligned Lombok and Kotlin versions in shared BOM; added CI version-check gate.

description: NullPointerException storm on batch-reconciliation after promotion to production. A transitive dependency pulled in Guava 32 which changed the behaviour of ImmutableList.copyOf when given a null-containing collection; the UAT environment's pinned version (31) did not exhibit this.
error_signature: java.lang.NullPointerException: ImmutableList.copyOf does not accept null elements
root_cause: Guava 32 changed ImmutableList.copyOf null handling; UAT pinned to Guava 31.
resolution: Pinned Guava version in BOM; added null-element test cases to regression suite.

description: Message lag on the payment-processor Kafka topic reached 4 million events. A misconfigured consumer group caused two consumer pods to rebalance in an infinite loop, never committing offsets.
root_cause: Misconfigured consumer group caused infinite rebalance loop.
resolution: Fixed consumer group config; added liveness probe to kill stalled consumers.

description: RabbitMQ cluster lost quorum after two of three nodes failed during a simultaneous patch window. interbank-connector could not connect to the broker and began buffering messages locally until disk was exhausted.
root_cause: RabbitMQ lost quorum after simultaneous patch of two nodes.
resolution: Restored RabbitMQ quorum by staggering node patches; implemented rolling-patch policy.

description: Transaction failures on customer-api traced to an expired client certificate used by the service account calling the HSM API. The HSM rejected all signing requests, causing payment authorisation to time out.
root_cause: Expired HSM client certificate caused all signing requests to be rejected.
resolution: Renewed the HSM client certificate via PKI portal; updated the Kubernetes secret and triggered a rolling restart.

description: loan-origination passed all UAT tests but failed immediately after prod deployment. Investigation revealed that the prod environment still ran jackson-databind 2.13.x while the new build required 2.15.x for a security patch; UAT had the newer version installed.
root_cause: Prod ran jackson-databind 2.13 but new build required 2.15; UAT had 2.15.
resolution: Pinned jackson-databind to 2.15 in prod base image; added dependency matrix tests.

description: interbank-connector publish latency spiked from 5 ms to 45 s. The broker's JVM heap was exhausted because a large batch publish from the reporting service was not rate-limited and created GC pressure.
error_signature: BrokerNotAvailableException: The server disconnected before a response was received
root_cause: Unthrottled batch publish exhausted broker JVM heap, triggering GC storm.
resolution: Added publish rate limiter to reporting service; increased broker heap to 32 GB.

description: Gradual increase in TLS negotiation latency on customer-api over 48 hours. Caused by a cipher-suite mismatch after a load balancer firmware upgrade disabled TLS 1.2 fallback; some legacy clients couldn't complete the TLS 1.3 handshake.
root_cause: Load balancer firmware upgrade disabled TLS 1.2, breaking legacy clients.
resolution: Restored TLS 1.2 on the load balancer for the interim; scheduled cipher suite migration project to upgrade legacy clients.

description: Mutual TLS authentication between fraud-detection and the downstream payments-gateway service broke after a Kubernetes secret rotation. The new cert was issued by a different intermediate CA not present in the client trust store.
error_signature: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
root_cause: Kubernetes secret rotation introduced new intermediate CA not in client trust store.
resolution: Added the new intermediate CA to the client trust store; rolled out updated ConfigMap to all affected namespaces. Documented trust-store update as a mandatory step in the cert-rotation runbook.

description: Production rollout of kyc-service triggered ClassCastException deep in the HTTP client stack. The application server's provided Netty version (4.1.86) conflicted with the version bundled in the service JAR (4.1.100); class loading order differed between UAT and prod app servers.
root_cause: Netty version in app server (4.1.86) conflicted with bundled version (4.1.100).
resolution: Excluded bundled Netty from fat-JAR; aligned app server and service versions.

description: Batch job in api-gateway aborted with 'IncompatibleClassChangeError'. A Lombok annotation processor version bump (1.18.26 → 1.18.30) generated code incompatible with the Kotlin version used in the same Maven module; UAT was on a different Kotlin version.
root_cause: Lombok and Kotlin version mismatch caused incompatible generated code.
resolution: Aligned Lombok and Kotlin versions in shared BOM; added CI version-check gate.

description: Production rollout of kyc-service triggered ClassCastException deep in the HTTP client stack. The application server's provided Netty version (4.1.86) conflicted with the version bundled in the service JAR (4.1.100); class loading order differed between UAT and prod app servers.
root_cause: Netty version in app server (4.1.86) conflicted with bundled version (4.1.100).
resolution: Excluded bundled Netty from fat-JAR; aligned app server and service versions.

description: Silent data corruption in api-gateway outputs traced to a Protobuf version mismatch: the producer compiled schemas with protoc 3.21 but the consumer still used protoc 3.19; a field added in the new schema was silently dropped by the old deserialiser.
root_cause: Protobuf version mismatch between producer (3.21) and consumer (3.19).
resolution: Pinned protoc version in CI; added schema-compatibility check between producer and consumer.

description: trade-settlement experienced a complete message processing halt. A poison-pill message with a malformed timestamp caused the deserialisation layer to throw and the consumer to exit without re-queuing.
error_signature: KafkaException: Topic partition leader not available
root_cause: Poison-pill message crashed consumer without re-queue handling.
resolution: Added poison-pill handler: log and move to DLQ rather than crashing consumer.

description: Monitoring alerted on elevated TLS error rates for fraud-detection. The cert had been renewed correctly but the new cert chain included a cross-signed root removed from Mozilla NSS in a recent update; Java's cacerts bundle on prod had not been refreshed.
root_cause: Java cacerts bundle not updated after Mozilla NSS root removal.
resolution: Updated Java cacerts bundle on all prod nodes; scripted the bundle refresh into the OS image pipeline so future kernel/JDK patches include it.

description: Interbroker replication between RabbitMQ partitions stalled after a network partition in the DR data centre. trade-settlement consumers were directed to the isolated partition and received stale data.
error_signature: javax.jms.JMSException: MQJMS2005: failed to create MQQueueManager
root_cause: Network partition isolated DR broker partition; consumers received stale data.
resolution: Updated routing rules to avoid isolated partition; scheduled DR network fix.

description: kyc-service failed health checks after deployment. OpenAPI spec validation library springdoc-openapi was upgraded, and the new version required Spring Boot 3.x; the service still ran Spring Boot 2.7.x in prod, whereas UAT had already been migrated.
root_cause: springdoc-openapi upgrade required Spring Boot 3.x; prod still on 2.7.x.
resolution: Upgraded prod to Spring Boot 3.x; added Spring version matrix to CI.

description: The card-processing service started throwing SSL handshake failures shortly after midnight. Certificate for internal CA expired at 00:05 UTC and was not auto-renewed because the ACME client lost connectivity to the CA server during a routine network maintenance window.
root_cause: Expired internal CA certificate; ACME renewal failure during maintenance window.
resolution: Renewed the internal CA cert via ACME; restarted the affected service pods. Added cert-expiry Prometheus alert with 30-day warning threshold.

description: customer-api health-check endpoint returned 503 with 'peer certificate: No certificate signed by known CA'. A new firewall appliance inline inspection had broken TLS by presenting its own self-signed cert to backend services.
root_cause: Firewall inline TLS inspection presented self-signed cert to backends.
resolution: Configured the firewall appliance in transparent mode for internal east-west traffic. Whitelisted the internal CA on the appliance policy.

description: Gradual increase in TLS negotiation latency on fraud-detection over 48 hours. Caused by a cipher-suite mismatch after a load balancer firmware upgrade disabled TLS 1.2 fallback; some legacy clients couldn't complete the TLS 1.3 handshake.
root_cause: Load balancer firmware upgrade disabled TLS 1.2, breaking legacy clients.
resolution: Restored TLS 1.2 on the load balancer for the interim; scheduled cipher suite migration project to upgrade legacy clients.

description: interbank-connector experienced a complete message processing halt. A poison-pill message with a malformed timestamp caused the deserialisation layer to throw and the consumer to exit without re-queuing.
error_signature: KafkaException: Topic partition leader not available
root_cause: Poison-pill message crashed consumer without re-queue handling.
resolution: Added poison-pill handler: log and move to DLQ rather than crashing consumer.

description: High CPU on the Kafka broker cluster caused throughput to drop 95 percent. Root cause: a compaction job and a schema-registry sync both ran concurrently during peak hours due to misconfigured cron.
root_cause: Compaction + schema-registry jobs ran concurrently causing CPU saturation.
resolution: Separated compaction and schema-sync cron jobs; added CPU-based backoff.

description: payment-processor publish latency spiked from 5 ms to 45 s. The broker's JVM heap was exhausted because a large batch publish from the reporting service was not rate-limited and created GC pressure.
error_signature: BrokerNotAvailableException: The server disconnected before a response was received
root_cause: Unthrottled batch publish exhausted broker JVM heap, triggering GC storm.
resolution: Added publish rate limiter to reporting service; increased broker heap to 32 GB.

description: Connection pool to RabbitMQ on fraud-event-stream was exhausted after a connection leak introduced in version 2.4.1 of the internal MQ client library. Connections were not returned after transient errors.
error_signature: ConnectionPoolTimeoutError: no available connections in pool
root_cause: Connection leak in MQ client library v2.4.1 exhausted broker connection pool.
resolution: Upgraded MQ client library to v2.4.2 with connection-leak fix; added pool monitoring.

description: Message lag on the interbank-connector Kafka topic reached 4 million events. A misconfigured consumer group caused two consumer pods to rebalance in an infinite loop, never committing offsets.
root_cause: Misconfigured consumer group caused infinite rebalance loop.
resolution: Fixed consumer group config; added liveness probe to kill stalled consumers.

description: Engineers observed a spike in 'certificate verify failed' errors from payments-gateway. Investigation revealed the wildcard cert covering *.internal.bank had reached its 398-day browser limit; the cert rotation job had silently failed two weeks earlier due to a missing Vault token.
root_cause: Wildcard cert hit 398-day limit; silent Vault token failure blocked renewal.
resolution: Re-issued wildcard cert and repaired the Vault token rotation job. Deployed cert-manager CRD to automate future renewals.

description: risk-scoring reported 'NoSuchMethodError' in prod. The commons-lang3 library was upgraded from 3.11 to 3.14 in the shared platform layer, but the risk-scoring JAR still referenced the old method signature because it was compiled against 3.11 and the fat-JAR was stale.
root_cause: commons-lang3 upgraded in shared layer; service JAR compiled against old version.
resolution: Rebuilt fat-JAR against commons-lang3 3.14; added shared-layer upgrade notification process.

description: Consumer offset reset for notification-dispatcher topic was accidentally triggered in production by a script intended for the staging environment, causing the consumer to replay 48 hours of messages and overwhelm downstream.
error_signature: OffsetOutOfRangeError: offset out of range for topic partition
root_cause: Staging offset-reset script accidentally executed against production topic.
resolution: Rolled back the offset; added environment guard to reset script.

description: card-processing began rejecting inbound connections from the batch settlement service. Client cert presented by the batch job expired; the automated renewal had been disabled by a config drift during last quarter's infrastructure-as-code migration.
root_cause: Cert renewal automation disabled by infrastructure-as-code config drift.
resolution: Re-enabled cert renewal automation in Terraform; triggered an immediate renewal cycle; updated drift-detection policy to flag disabled renewal.

description: Post-deployment smoke tests for card-processing reported HTTP 526 ('invalid SSL certificate'). Root cause: the deployment pipeline overwrote the prod cert with a staging cert during an environment-copy step.
error_signature: PKIX path building failed: unable to find valid certification path
root_cause: Deployment pipeline overwrote prod cert with staging cert.
resolution: Corrected the deployment pipeline's environment-copy step to skip secret overwrite. Promoted the correct prod cert from the secrets manager.

description: The mobile-banking-backend service started throwing SSL handshake failures shortly after midnight. Certificate for internal CA expired at 00:05 UTC and was not auto-renewed because the ACME client lost connectivity to the CA server during a routine network maintenance window.
root_cause: Expired internal CA certificate; ACME renewal failure during maintenance window.
resolution: Renewed the internal CA cert via ACME; restarted the affected service pods. Added cert-expiry Prometheus alert with 30-day warning threshold.

description: Mutual TLS authentication between customer-api and the downstream payments-gateway service broke after a Kubernetes secret rotation. The new cert was issued by a different intermediate CA not present in the client trust store.
error_signature: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
root_cause: Kubernetes secret rotation introduced new intermediate CA not in client trust store.
resolution: Added the new intermediate CA to the client trust store; rolled out updated ConfigMap to all affected namespaces. Documented trust-store update as a mandatory step in the cert-rotation runbook.

description: reporting-engine passed all UAT tests but failed immediately after prod deployment. Investigation revealed that the prod environment still ran jackson-databind 2.13.x while the new build required 2.15.x for a security patch; UAT had the newer version installed.
root_cause: Prod ran jackson-databind 2.13 but new build required 2.15; UAT had 2.15.
resolution: Pinned jackson-databind to 2.15 in prod base image; added dependency matrix tests.

description: payment-processor stopped consuming messages from the RabbitMQ queue. The broker had run out of disk space after a dead-letter queue grew unbounded following an upstream schema change that made all messages unparseable.
root_cause: Dead-letter queue grew unbounded after schema change; broker disk exhausted.
resolution: Purged dead-letter queue; added DLQ depth alert and schema-validation step.

description: Stack overflow errors on risk-scoring startup after a Hibernate upgrade from 5.6 to 6.1 in the shared ORM layer. The new version changed proxy class generation; a circular @ManyToMany mapping caused infinite recursion only with the Hibernate 6 proxy strategy.
root_cause: Hibernate 6 proxy strategy caused StackOverflow on circular @ManyToMany mapping.
resolution: Fixed @ManyToMany mapping to use join table; added Hibernate version gate to upgrade process.

description: Monitoring alerted on elevated TLS error rates for mobile-banking-backend. The cert had been renewed correctly but the new cert chain included a cross-signed root removed from Mozilla NSS in a recent update; Java's cacerts bundle on prod had not been refreshed.

resolution: Updated Java cacerts bundle on all prod nodes; scripted the bundle refresh into the OS image pipeline so future kernel/JDK patches include it.

resolution: Corrected the deployment pipeline's environment-copy step to skip secret overwrite. Promoted the correct prod cert from the secrets manager.

root_cause: Protobuf version mismatch between producer (3.21) and consumer (3.19).
resolution: Pinned protoc version in CI; added schema-compatibility check between producer and consumer.

error_signature: javax.jms.JMSException: MQJMS2005: failed to create MQQueueManager
root_cause: Network partition isolated DR broker partition; consumers received stale data.
resolution: Updated routing rules to avoid isolated partition; scheduled DR network fix.

root_cause: Wildcard cert hit 398-day limit; silent Vault token failure blocked renewal.
resolution: Re-issued wildcard cert and repaired the Vault token rotation job. Deployed cert-manager CRD to automate future renewals.

description: Batch job in kyc-service aborted with 'IncompatibleClassChangeError'. A Lombok annotation processor version bump (1.18.26 → 1.18.30) generated code incompatible with the Kotlin version used in the same Maven module; UAT was on a different Kotlin version.
root_cause: Lombok and Kotlin version mismatch caused incompatible generated code.
resolution: Aligned Lombok and Kotlin versions in shared BOM; added CI version-check gate.

description: Silent data corruption in reporting-engine outputs traced to a Protobuf version mismatch: the producer compiled schemas with protoc 3.21 but the consumer still used protoc 3.19; a field added in the new schema was silently dropped by the old deserialiser.
root_cause: Protobuf version mismatch between producer (3.21) and consumer (3.19).
resolution: Pinned protoc version in CI; added schema-compatibility check between producer and consumer.

description: RabbitMQ cluster lost quorum after two of three nodes failed during a simultaneous patch window. interbank-connector could not connect to the broker and began buffering messages locally until disk was exhausted.
root_cause: RabbitMQ lost quorum after simultaneous patch of two nodes.
resolution: Restored RabbitMQ quorum by staggering node patches; implemented rolling-patch policy.
